elements.sent-2411-001: We develop a comprehensive analytic framework for jet quenching in QCD media, based on a medium-induced parton cascade sourced by collinear virtual splittings.
elements.sent-2411-002: We show that the energy flow out of the jet cone, driven by turbulent gluon cascades, is governed by a non-linear rate equation that resums gluon splittings at arbitrary angles and is enhanced by the medium length, $L$.
elements.sent-2411-003: The solution of this equation sets the initial condition for a non-linear DGLAP-like evolution equation, which describes the collinear early vacuum cascade resolved by the medium at angles exceeding the medium resolution angle, $\theta_c$.
elements.sent-2411-004: For asymptotic jet energies, the medium-induced cascade displays an exponential behavior that generalizes the Poisson-like distribution of parton energy loss.
elements.sent-2411-005: This formulation enables the resummation of leading contributions in $\alpha_s \ln (1/R)$, and $\alpha_s \ln (R / \theta_c)$, and powers of $\alpha_s L$.
elements.sent-2411-006: We briefly explore the limit of strong quenching, where analytic treatments are feasible, offering insights into the impact of parton cascades on jet quenching.
elements.sent-2411-007: These results provide guidance for future numerical simulations and analytical investigations.

elements.sent-2411-017: The primary mechanism driving jet quenching is parton energy loss, which occurs through elastic scattering and radiation processes.
elements.sent-2411-018: The latter, gluon radiation, becomes equally important in large media, as the coupling constant factor associated with radiation is enhanced (on average) by the square of the medium length, $L$ \cite{Wang:1991xy,Gyulassy:1990ye,Wang:1992qdg,Gyulassy:2000fs,Baier:1994bd,Baier:1996kr,Zakharov:1996fv,Zakharov:1997uu,Gyulassy:2000er,Wiedemann:1999fq,Baier:2001yt,Wiedemann:2000za,Arnold:2002ja,Salgado:2003gb .}

elements.sent-2411-036: This article is organized as follows: In Section~\ref{sec:eloss-dist , we establish the general factorization formula for the inclusive jet cross-section and introduce the normalized energy loss distribution.}
elements.sent-2411-037: In Section~\ref{sec:med-shower , we derive the non-linear master equation governing the evolution of the energy loss distribution through the medium-induced gluon cascade.}
elements.sent-2411-038: In Section~\ref{sec:vacuum , we discuss the impact of the early collinear cascade on energy loss, described by a non-linear DGLAP equation.}
elements.sent-2411-039: Section~\ref{sec:steep-spect provides an analytical analysis of the jet spectrum, highlighting that in the strong quenching regime, multi-subjet configurations are significantly suppressed by a Sudakov-like factor.}
elements.sent-2411-040: Finally, in Section~\ref{sec:NP-effects , we address the inclusion of soft physics occurring at the medium temperature scale through a non-perturbative factor, enabling a simple analytic treatment of medium response.}
elements.sent-2411-041: Finally, we address the generalization to higher orders in Section~\ref{sec:higher-orders and present our conclusions in Section~\ref{sec:concl .}}

elements.sent-2411-070: In vacuum, only collinear splittings at angles larger than $R \ll 1$ produce significant logarithmic contributions, i.e., $\alpha_s \ln (1/R) \sim 1$.
elements.sent-2411-071: Since only the total transverse momentum ($p_T$) of the jet is measured, the observable is insensitive to fluctuations in the jet substructure \cite{Kang:2016mcy,Dasgupta:2014yra .}

elements.sent-2411-073: In contrast, a QCD medium resolves different substructure fluctuations, leading to differential energy loss that impacts the observable.
elements.sent-2411-074: For example, a jet that splits into two subjets will experience more energy loss than one that does not split, causing an imbalance between real and virtual contributions.

elements.sent-2411-248: In this work, we developed a comprehensive analytic framework for computing the inclusive jet spectrum in heavy-ion collisions, with a focus on the non-linear dynamics driven by medium-induced parton cascades.
elements.sent-2411-249: Our approach captures the intricate interplay between collinear vacuum cascades and turbulent gluon cascades initiated by medium interactions, offering valuable insights into the redistribution of energy both within and outside the jet cone.
elements.sent-2411-250: By systematically resumming leading contributions in parameters such as $\alpha_s \ln(1/R)$, $\alpha_s \ln(R/\theta_c)$, and powers of $\alpha_s L$, our framework extends the factorization derived in \cite{Mehtar-Tani:2024smp and generalizes the non-linear evolution equations for jet quenching proposed in \cite{Mehtar-Tani:2017web .}}
elements.sent-2411-251: Furthermore, we highlighted the dual role of the medium in jet evolution: resolving substructure fluctuations while inducing suppression of micro-jets within the jet cone.
elements.sent-2411-252: This framework, accounts for coherence effects, medium response, and Sudakov suppression in jet quenching.
elements.sent-2411-253: Our formulation provides a basis for improving precision studies of jet quenching, allowing for the inclusion of higher-order corrections and non-perturbative effects.
elements.sent-2411-254: In addition to inclusive jet cross-sections, it may be applicable to jet substructure observables, enabling a more differential probe of in-medium jet modifications.
elements.sent-2411-255: This new framework offers a promising path forward for future numerical simulations and phenomenological applications, advancing our understanding of jet quenching in heavy-ion collisions.
